quiz_questions.question_text: What does the y-axis represent on a velocity-time graph?

options.text: The velocity of the object
The position of the object
The acceleration of the object
The total distance traveled

quiz_questions.explanation: On a velocity-time graph, the y-axis shows the velocity of the object at each moment. The x-axis shows time. This lets us see how fast the object is moving at any point during its journey.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does a horizontal line on a velocity-time graph indicate about the object's motion?

options.text: The object is speeding up steadily
The object is moving at constant velocity
The object is slowing down steadily
The object is completely at rest

quiz_questions.explanation: A horizontal (flat) line on a velocity-time graph means the velocity is not changing. The object moves at a constant velocity with zero acceleration. A flat line at zero would mean at rest, but any other flat line means steady motion.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does the slope of a line on a velocity-time graph represent?

options.text: The total displacement of the object
The average speed of the object
The acceleration of the object
The direction the object faces

quiz_questions.explanation: The slope of a velocity-time graph equals the rate at which velocity changes, which is acceleration. A steeper slope means greater acceleration, and a negative slope means the object is decelerating.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does the area under a velocity-time graph represent?

options.text: The average velocity of the object
The total force on the object
The acceleration of the object
The displacement of the object

quiz_questions.explanation: The area between the velocity-time line and the time axis equals the displacement. For a rectangle (constant velocity), area = velocity x time = displacement. This works for any shape on the graph.

quiz_questions.question_text: On a velocity-time graph, what does a straight line with a positive slope indicate?

options.text: Constant positive acceleration
Decreasing positive velocity
Constant negative acceleration
No acceleration at all

quiz_questions.explanation: A straight line with a positive slope on a v-t graph means the velocity is increasing at a steady rate. This is uniform positive acceleration. The steeper the line, the greater the acceleration.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does a line sloping downward on a velocity-time graph tell you?

options.text: The object is gaining speed over time
The object is moving at a steady pace
The object is slowing down over time
The object is not moving at all

quiz_questions.explanation: A downward-sloping line means velocity is decreasing over time. The object is decelerating (slowing down). If the line crosses zero, the object stops and may reverse direction.

quiz_questions.question_text: On a velocity-time graph, a steeper slope compared to a gentler slope means what?

options.text: The object has a lower velocity
The object has a greater acceleration
The object covers less total distance
The object has exactly zero acceleration

quiz_questions.explanation: Since slope on a velocity-time graph equals acceleration, a steeper slope means the velocity is changing more rapidly. A steep upward slope is large acceleration; a gentle slope is small acceleration.

quiz_questions.question_text: What are the standard units shown on the axes of a velocity-time graph?

options.text: Meters on the y-axis and kilograms on the x-axis
Newtons on the y-axis and seconds on the x-axis
Meters per second on the y-axis and meters on x-axis
Meters per second on the y-axis and seconds on x-axis

quiz_questions.explanation: A velocity-time graph plots velocity (m/s) on the y-axis against time (s) on the x-axis. These are the standard SI units used for this type of motion graph.

quiz_questions.question_text: On a velocity-time graph, what does a line sitting at zero velocity for a period of time mean?

options.text: The object is completely at rest during that time
The object is accelerating very quickly upward
The object is traveling at maximum possible speed
The object is moving at a constant slow speed

quiz_questions.explanation: If the line on a velocity-time graph stays at zero, the object has zero velocity and is not moving. It is completely at rest for that entire time interval.

quiz_questions.question_text: When a velocity-time graph line crosses the zero velocity line, what happens to the object?

options.text: The object starts to accelerate faster
The object changes its direction of motion
The object comes to a permanent stop
The object doubles its current speed

quiz_questions.explanation: When the line crosses zero velocity on a v-t graph, the object momentarily stops and then reverses direction. Positive velocity becomes negative, meaning the object now moves in the opposite direction.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a velocity-time graph?

options.text: A table listing speed values at different locations
A diagram showing forces acting on an object
A graph that plots an object's velocity against time
A chart comparing distance to total mass

quiz_questions.explanation: A velocity-time graph (v-t graph) is a visual tool that plots an object's velocity on the y-axis against time on the x-axis. It shows how the speed and direction of an object change over a period of time.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does the term 'acceleration' mean on a velocity-time graph?

options.text: The total distance covered by an object over time
The object's position at a single instant in time
The weight of the moving object at any moment
The rate at which velocity changes, shown by slope

quiz_questions.explanation: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity. On a velocity-time graph, it is represented by the slope of the line. A positive slope means speeding up, a negative slope means slowing down, and zero slope means constant velocity.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'deceleration' mean?

options.text: Motion in a perfectly circular path
A decrease in an object's velocity over time
An increase in velocity over a time period
Maintaining the exact same speed always

quiz_questions.explanation: Deceleration means the object is slowing down, so its velocity is decreasing over time. On a velocity-time graph, deceleration appears as a line with a negative slope going downward.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'constant velocity' mean?

options.text: Speed and direction remain the same over time
Speed increases at a steady and uniform rate
The object frequently changes its direction
The object is not moving in any direction

quiz_questions.explanation: Constant velocity means both the speed and direction of an object stay the same. On a velocity-time graph, this appears as a flat horizontal line because the velocity does not change.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does a negative velocity value mean on a velocity-time graph?

options.text: The object has completely stopped moving
The object is getting heavier over time
The object is accelerating very rapidly
The object is moving in the opposite direction

quiz_questions.explanation: A negative velocity on a v-t graph means the object is moving in the direction opposite to what was defined as positive. It does not mean the object is stopped; it simply means it is traveling the other way.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'uniform acceleration' mean?

options.text: The object moves in a circular path always
Velocity stays constant without any changes
Velocity changes by equal amounts in equal times
The object stops and then reverses itself

quiz_questions.explanation: Uniform acceleration means the velocity changes at a constant rate. On a velocity-time graph, uniform acceleration appears as a straight line because the velocity increases or decreases by the same amount every second.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is meant by 'displacement' when reading a velocity-time graph?

options.text: The total area between the line and the time axis
The highest point reached on the velocity axis
The slope of the line at any particular point
The difference between the start and end time

quiz_questions.explanation: Displacement is the change in position of an object. On a velocity-time graph, displacement equals the area between the line and the time axis. Areas above the axis count as positive, and areas below count as negative.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'instantaneous velocity' mean on a velocity-time graph?

options.text: The average of all velocity values on the graph
The velocity at one specific moment shown on the graph
The final velocity minus the initial velocity value
The maximum velocity the object can ever reach

quiz_questions.explanation: Instantaneous velocity is the velocity of an object at one specific point in time. On a velocity-time graph, you can read it directly by finding the value on the y-axis at any chosen time on the x-axis.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'non-uniform acceleration' mean?

options.text: The velocity stays exactly the same over time
The velocity increases by equal amounts each second
The object is completely stationary at all times
The acceleration changes, shown as a curved line

quiz_questions.explanation: Non-uniform acceleration means the rate of velocity change is itself changing. On a v-t graph, this appears as a curved line rather than a straight one, because the slope is different at different points.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the meaning of 'slope' on a velocity-time graph?

options.text: The total distance an object has traveled
The mass of the object that is in motion
The steepness of the line showing how fast velocity changes
The point where the line crosses the y-axis

quiz_questions.explanation: The slope of a line is its steepness, calculated as rise over run. On a velocity-time graph, the slope equals the change in velocity divided by the change in time, which is the definition of acceleration.

quiz_questions.question_text: A car starts from rest and reaches 20 m/s in 4 seconds, shown as a straight line on a v-t graph. What is its acceleration?

options.text: 80 m/s squared
5 m/s squared
24 m/s squared
16 m/s squared

quiz_questions.explanation: Acceleration equals the slope of the v-t graph: change in velocity divided by change in time. Here, acceleration = (20 - 0) / (4 - 0) = 20/4 = 5 m/s squared.

quiz_questions.question_text: A train's v-t graph shows a horizontal line at 30 m/s for 10 seconds. How far did the train travel during that time?

options.text: 300 meters
150 meters
400 meters
200 meters

quiz_questions.explanation: The area under a v-t graph equals displacement. For a horizontal line at 30 m/s over 10 seconds, the area is a rectangle: 30 m/s x 10 s = 300 meters.

quiz_questions.question_text: A bicycle's v-t graph shows velocity dropping from 15 m/s to 0 m/s in 5 seconds. What is the rate of deceleration?

options.text: 75 m/s squared
20 m/s squared
10 m/s squared
3 m/s squared

quiz_questions.explanation: Deceleration = change in velocity / time = (15 - 0) / 5 = 3 m/s squared. The velocity decreased by 3 m/s every second until the bicycle came to a complete stop.

quiz_questions.question_text: A runner's v-t graph shows 8 m/s for 5 seconds, then 4 m/s for 5 seconds. What is the total displacement?

options.text: 40 meters total
20 meters total
60 meters total
80 meters total

quiz_questions.explanation: Displacement equals the total area under the v-t graph. First section: 8 m/s x 5 s = 40 m. Second section: 4 m/s x 5 s = 20 m. Total displacement = 40 + 20 = 60 meters.

quiz_questions.question_text: A rocket accelerates uniformly from 0 to 100 m/s in 10 seconds. What shape would its v-t graph be?

options.text: A straight line sloping upward from zero
A flat horizontal line at 100 m/s
A curved line bending downward sharply
A straight line sloping downward to zero

quiz_questions.explanation: Uniform (constant) acceleration produces a straight line on a v-t graph. Since the rocket goes from 0 to 100 m/s, the line starts at the origin and slopes upward at a constant rate.

quiz_questions.question_text: A car's v-t graph goes from +10 m/s to -10 m/s over several seconds. What happened to the car?

options.text: The car sped up to twice its speed
The car slowed, stopped, and reversed direction
The car maintained a constant velocity
The car remained completely stationary

quiz_questions.explanation: Going from +10 m/s to -10 m/s means the car was moving forward, slowed to zero (stopped momentarily), and then began moving backward. The sign change indicates a reversal of direction.

quiz_questions.question_text: A skateboarder's v-t graph shows a curved line that gets steeper over time. What does this tell you about the motion?

options.text: The skateboarder is slowing down gradually
The skateboarder is moving at constant speed
The skateboarder is completely stationary now
The skateboarder's acceleration is increasing

quiz_questions.explanation: A curve that gets steeper over time means the slope is increasing. Since slope equals acceleration on a v-t graph, an increasingly steep curve means the acceleration is getting larger over time.

quiz_questions.question_text: Two runners have v-t graphs that both start from rest. Runner A's line is steeper than Runner B's. What does this tell you?

options.text: Runner B is accelerating much faster
Both runners have exactly the same speed
Runner A has a greater acceleration than Runner B
Runner A is decelerating while Runner B speeds up

quiz_questions.explanation: A steeper slope on a v-t graph means a larger acceleration. Since Runner A's line is steeper, Runner A is speeding up faster than Runner B. Runner A gains velocity more quickly each second.

quiz_questions.question_text: A bus drives for 20 seconds and the area under its v-t graph is 500 m. What does the 500 m represent?

options.text: The bus's acceleration during the trip
The total displacement of the bus
The top speed the bus reached at peak
The total weight of the bus in motion

quiz_questions.explanation: The area under a velocity-time graph always represents displacement. So 500 m is the total displacement of the bus during its 20-second journey.

quiz_questions.question_text: A ball rolling uphill has a v-t graph that starts at 6 m/s and drops to 0 m/s in 3 seconds. What is the total displacement?

options.text: 9 meters
6 meters
3 meters
18 meters

quiz_questions.explanation: The area under the v-t graph is a triangle with base 3 s and height 6 m/s. Area of a triangle = 1/2 x base x height = 1/2 x 3 x 6 = 9 meters. The ball traveled 9 meters uphill before stopping.
